evals.name: cross-feature-import
evals.expected_output: Flags the cross-feature imports as a High Cohesion / Low Coupling violation. features/billing imports from features/auth and features/teams — this creates coupling between feature modules. Suggests moving shared logic (getUserById, getTeamMembers) to core/ or exposing a service interface through shared/.

assertions.description: Flags the imports from features/auth and features/teams as a coupling violation
References the import rule that features/A cannot import from features/B
Suggests moving shared dependencies to core/ or shared/ to fix the coupling
Does NOT approve the commit — requires the violation to be fixed first

evals.name: todo-marker-detection
evals.expected_output: Flags both markers: FIXME (incomplete validation) and HACK (ORM bypass). Both violate Fail Fast — deferred problems must be fixed before committing, not marked for later. Demands the validation be completed and the ORM issue resolved or documented in an ADR.

assertions.description: Flags the FIXME comment about incomplete validation
Flags the HACK comment about bypassing the ORM
References Fail Fast philosophy — no TODO/FIXME/HACK in committed code
Does NOT approve the commit — requires both issues to be fixed first

evals.name: undocumented-dependency
evals.expected_output: Flags the undocumented dependency. Adding lodash is an architectural decision (new external dependency) that requires an ADR via /propose-architecture. Questions whether the full lodash library is needed for a single merge operation — YAGNI applies. Suggests structuredClone + spread or a targeted package like lodash.merge.

assertions.description: Flags that lodash was added without an ADR or architectural decision record
Suggests using /propose-architecture to document the dependency decision
Questions whether the full lodash library is needed for one merge operation — YAGNI concern
Suggests a simpler alternative (e.g., structuredClone, spread operator, or lodash.merge)
